entries.note: The ControlMatrix component was already fully implemented in a prior session at src/web/components/ControlMatrix/ControlMatrix.tsx and wired into WorkflowDetailRoute. The component uses a declarative RULES object mapping each action to a predicate over (workflowStatus, selectedItem, activeStage), generates commandIds via crypto.randomUUID(), shows optimistic spinner state until 10s timeout, presents a controlled confirmation dialog for destructive actions (cancel, skip), and opens a text-input modal for inject-context. This session added comprehensive e2e test coverage in control-matrix.spec.ts (19 tests). Tests cover: AC-2 (pause sends control frame with action=pause; commandId is in the frame's top-level id field as a UUID); AC-3 (cancel requires confirmation dialog, then sends frame); AC-4 (optimistic disabled state on pause and resume after click); AC-5 (commandId is a UUID via crypto.randomUUID, verified via frame.id); AC-6 (inject-context opens modal, Inject button disabled while empty, cancel closes without sending, filled text sent as extra); AC-7 (approve-stage visible only when stage.needsApproval=true AND stage.status=complete, hidden for in_progress or needsApproval=false, sends stageId in frame); AC-1/AC-8 (actions update dynamically via workflow.update, cancel hidden for complete workflow, no toolbar when no active session). Item-scoped action tests cover skip/unblock for blocked items, retry for failed items, hidden when no item selected, skip confirmation dialog sends itemId, and dynamic update when item.state frame transitions the selected item.
deferred_criteria: RC-4: rerun-phase visibility — rerun-phase requires item.state.status in ['complete','failed'] and there is no separate test for it beyond retry (failed). The rule is in RULES and is covered implicitly, but no explicit test for 'complete' item showing 'Rerun phase' button was written. Low risk since the rule is symmetric with retry.

AC-5 idempotency (same commandId on retry within 5 min) — the client-side idempotency cache reuses commandId for repeated clicks within 5 minutes. Not tested via e2e because Playwright cannot easily assert that two rapid clicks produce identical frame.id values without timing control.
known_risks: Item-selection tests rely on clicking #item-<itemId> in the FeatureBoard panel. If FeatureBoard changes its item ID scheme or the item card becomes unclickable (e.g., clickable region changes), item-scoped ControlMatrix tests will silently miss the selectedItem update and fail to see skip/retry/unblock buttons.

The 10s optimistic timeout (setTimeout in ControlMatrix.handleAction) re-enables buttons after 10s regardless of server ack. The spinner-disabled tests pass because they check immediately after click, before 10s elapses. On very slow CI this is fine, but if a test somehow waited 10+ seconds, the button would re-enable and the toBeDisabled assertion would fail.

approve-stage active-stage lookup uses stages.find(s => s.status === 'in_progress' || s.status === 'complete'). If multiple stages match, the first one wins. Tests use single-stage scenarios; multi-stage ordering edge cases are not covered.
